subtitle: Mid-back tightness from posture and fatigue

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Upper back fatigue common in the final third of marathon-distance runs

mild.description: Tightness between shoulder blades after runs, eases with stretching
mild.can_run_note: Yes — focus on tall posture and relaxed upper body
why: Rhomboid and mid-trapezius fatigue from sustained arm swing
Thoracic kyphosis increasing load on posterior structures
Weak scapular stabilisers allowing shoulders to round under fatigue
relief: Thoracic foam roller extension — 10 slow rolls post-run
Thread-the-needle stretch on all fours 30s each side
Heat pad between shoulder blades 15 min
prevention: Prone Y-T-W exercises 3× per week for scapular strength
Run tall — imagine a string pulling the crown of your head upward
Limit consecutive hours of sitting with rounded back

moderate.description: Persistent ache between shoulder blades, worse sitting after runs
moderate.can_run_note: Yes — shorten runs and focus on posture
why: Chronic thoracic stiffness reducing spinal extension during running
Rhomboid strain from repeated scapular retraction effort
Desk posture compounding running posture faults
relief: Thoracic manipulation or self-release on foam roller
Band rows and face pulls to strengthen mid-back
prevention: Thoracic mobility work daily — extension over foam roller
Incorporate upper back work into strength days

severe.description: Sharp localised pain, pain breathing deeply, cannot sit comfortably
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — rule out rib or thoracic joint issue first
why: Possible thoracic joint dysfunction or rib stress reaction
Referred pain that may need differential diagnosis
relief: Physiotherapy assessment — especially if pain with breathing
Avoid heavy lifting or overhead activity
GP review if no improvement in 5 days
prevention: Return only when full pain-free range of thoracic rotation restored

see_physio_if: Sharp localised pain rather than diffuse ache
